exercises.title: Create Cluster State
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def cluster_state_3():
    # Your code: |+>|+>|+> with CZ(0,1) and CZ(1,2)
    pass

state = cluster_state_3()
print(f"State has {len(state)} amplitudes")
print(f"Norm: {np.linalg.norm(state):.4f}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def cluster_state_3():
    # |+>^3
    state = np.ones(8) / np.sqrt(8)
    # CZ(0,1): flip sign when both qubits are 1
    for i in range(8):
        b0, b1 = (i >> 2) & 1, (i >> 1) & 1
        if b0 == 1 and b1 == 1:
            state[i] *= -1
    # CZ(1,2)
    for i in range(8):
        b1, b2 = (i >> 1) & 1, i & 1
        if b1 == 1 and b2 == 1:
            state[i] *= -1
    return state

state = cluster_state_3()
print(f"State: {state}")
print(f"Norm: {np.linalg.norm(state):.4f}")
hints: CZ flips sign when both qubits are |1>

exercises.title: Verify Entanglement
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def is_product_state(state, n_qubits):
    # Your code: check if state is separable
    pass

product = np.array([1, 0, 0, 0])  # |00>
cluster = np.array([1, 1, 1, -1]) / 2  # 2-qubit cluster
print(f"|00> product: {is_product_state(product, 2)}")
print(f"Cluster product: {is_product_state(cluster, 2)}")
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def is_product_state(state, n_qubits):
    # Reshape to matrix and check rank
    if n_qubits != 2:
        return None  # Only for 2 qubits
    mat = state.reshape(2, 2)
    rank = np.linalg.matrix_rank(mat, tol=1e-10)
    return rank == 1

product = np.array([1, 0, 0, 0], dtype=float)
cluster = np.array([1, 1, 1, -1], dtype=float) / 2
print(f"|00> product: {is_product_state(product, 2)}")
print(f"Cluster product: {is_product_state(cluster, 2)}")
